summary: Make citation relations text more clear

description.text: Current state: 1. Start JabRef 2. Click on "New exmaple library" 3. Select "Ding_2006" 6. Select tab "Citatoin relations" 7. See\ The text "Cites" and "Cited by" is confiusing. - Rename "Cites" to "References (cites)" - Add tooltip (on hover): "Works cited by the work at hand" - Rename "Cited by" to "Citations (this paper is cited by)" - Add tooltip (on hover): "Works citing the work at hand" See https://devdocs.jabref.org/code-howtos/localization.html for hints on localization strings.

Current state: 1. Start JabRef 2. Click on "New exmaple library" 3. Select "Ding_2006" 4. Select tab "Citation information" 5. See\ 6. Select tab "Citation relations" 7. See\ Since tab "Citation information" does not take much information, it can be merged into a new section within "Citation relations". If that new section requires a header, then name it "Metrics". Expected result: --- Task: 1. Rename tab "Citation relations" to "Citations" 1. Move org.jabref.gui.entryeditor.SciteTabViewModel#fetchTallies to new class `ScienceAiFetcher` in package `org.jabref.logic.importer.fetcher` (in module `jablib`. 2. Introduce `record` `TalliesResponse` (if handled by GSon, otherwise class) to handle `Response` from tallies endpoint 3. ViewModel should transform `TalliesResponse` to UI information It is OK to include in `CitationsRelationsTabViewModel`. You can also nest two JavaFX component in the tab "Citation relations" --- Notes: JSON response from view-source:https://api.scite.ai/tallies/10.1186%2F1743-7075-3-2 ```json {"total":77,"supporting":1,"contradicting":1,"mentioning":72,"unclassified":3,"doi":"10.1186/1743-7075-3-2","citingPublications":240} ``` For localization see https://devdocs.jabref.org/code-howtos/localization.html#localization-in-java-code

summary: [WIP] Fix UndoableMoveGroup redo operation causing "target cannot be a descendant" error

description.text:
Thanks for asking me to work on this. I will get started on it and keep this PR's description up to date as I form a plan and make progress.

Original description:

> # Fix UndoableMoveGroup redo operation causing "target cannot be a descendant" error
> 
> ## Problem Description
> 
> When moving a group with subgroups (like "By status" with children "Last read", "Skimmed", "To be read") to be a child of another group (like "For Project X"), the initial move and undo operations work correctly. However, when performing a redo operation, the system throws:
> 
> ```
> java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException: the target cannot be a descendant of this node
> 	at org.jabref.jablib/org.jabref.model.TreeNode.moveTo(TreeNode.java:552)
> 	at org.jabref/org.jabref.gui.groups.undo.UndoableMoveGroup.redo(UndoableMoveGroup.java:54)
> ```
> 
> ## Root Cause
> 
> The current `UndoableMoveGroup` implementation stores indexed paths (`pathToOldParent`, `pathToNewParent`) and child indices that become stale after tree modifications. When the redo operation tries to resolve these paths after an undo, the path resolution can lead to incorrect node references, causing the system to detect a circular reference where the node would become a descendant of itself.
> 
> The issue is in the `redo()` method:
> ```java
> GroupTreeNode oldParent = root.getGroupNode().getDescendant(pathToOldParent).get();
> GroupTreeNode node = oldParent.getChildAt(oldChildIndex).get();
> node.moveTo(root.getGroupNode().getDescendant(pathToNewParent).get(), newChildIndex);
> ```
> 
> After the undo operation, these paths may no longer point to the correct nodes.
> 
> ## Solution Required
> 
> Replace the path-based approach with direct node references to ensure the undo/redo operations work correctly regardless of tree structure changes. The fix should:
> 
> 1. Store direct references to the nodes involved in the move operation instead of paths
> 2. Ensure the undo and redo operations use these stable references
> 3. Maintain compatibility with the existing `MoveGroupChange` interface
> 4. Handle the case where nodes might be removed or relocated during complex operations
> 
> ## Example Test Case
> 
> 1. Create a group structure:
>    ```
>    - By status (with children: Last read, Skimmed, To be read)
>    - For Project X
>    ```
> 2. Move "By status" to be a child of "For Project X"
> 3. Perform undo (should work)
> 4. Perform redo (currently fails with the error)
> 
> The fix should ensure step 4 works correctly without throwing the descendant exception.
> 
> ## Files to Modify
> 
> - `src/main/java/org/jabref/gui/groups/undo/UndoableMoveGroup.java`
> - Potentially update the constructor to store node references instead of paths
> - Update both `undo()` and `redo()` methods to use direct node references

*This pull request was created as a result of the following prompt from Copilot chat.*
> # Fix UndoableMoveGroup redo operation causing "target cannot be a descendant" error
> 
> ## Problem Description
> 
> When moving a group with subgroups (like "By status" with children "Last read", "Skimmed", "To be read") to be a child of another group (like "For Project X"), the initial move and undo operations work correctly. However, when performing a redo operation, the system throws:
> 
> ```
> java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException: the target cannot be a descendant of this node
> 	at org.jabref.jablib/org.jabref.model.TreeNode.moveTo(TreeNode.java:552)
> 	at org.jabref/org.jabref.gui.groups.undo.UndoableMoveGroup.redo(UndoableMoveGroup.java:54)
> ```
> 
> ## Root Cause
> 
> The current `UndoableMoveGroup` implementation stores indexed paths (`pathToOldParent`, `pathToNewParent`) and child indices that become stale after tree modifications. When the redo operation tries to resolve these paths after an undo, the path resolution can lead to incorrect node references, causing the system to detect a circular reference where the node would become a descendant of itself.
> 
> The issue is in the `redo()` method:
> ```java
> GroupTreeNode oldParent = root.getGroupNode().getDescendant(pathToOldParent).get();
> GroupTreeNode node = oldParent.getChildAt(oldChildIndex).get();
> node.moveTo(root.getGroupNode().getDescendant(pathToNewParent).get(), newChildIndex);
> ```
> 
> After the undo operation, these paths may no longer point to the correct nodes.
> 
> ## Solution Required
> 
> Replace the path-based approach with direct node references to ensure the undo/redo operations work correctly regardless of tree structure changes. The fix should:
> 
> 1. Store direct references to the nodes involved in the move operation instead of paths
> 2. Ensure the undo and redo operations use these stable references
> 3. Maintain compatibility with the existing `MoveGroupChange` interface
> 4. Handle the case where nodes might be removed or relocated during complex operations
> 
> ## Example Test Case
> 
> 1. Create a group structure:
>    ```
>    - By status (with children: Last read, Skimmed, To be read)
>    - For Project X
>    ```
> 2. Move "By status" to be a child of "For Project X"
> 3. Perform undo (should work)
> 4. Perform redo (currently fails with the error)
> 
> The fix should ensure step 4 works correctly without throwing the descendant exception.
> 
> ## Files to Modify
> 
> - `src/main/java/org/jabref/gui/groups/undo/UndoableMoveGroup.java`
> - Potentially update the constructor to store node references instead of paths
> - Update both `undo()` and `redo()` methods to use direct node references

summary: Fix dark mode in {} of Citation Relations tab of the entry editor

description.text: Closes #13599 Fix dark mode in {} of Citation Relations tab of the entry editor. ### Steps to test 1. Switch to dark mode. 2. Open sample entry and go to Citation Relations tab. 3. Click on {} BibTeX source button. 4. Dialog shows content of the BibTeX source in the dark mode. 5. Switch back to the Light theme. ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [x] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [/] Tests created for changes (if applicable) - [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required) - [x] Screenshots added in PR description (if change is visible to the user) - [x] [Checked developer's documentation](https://devdocs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I outlined it in this pull request. - [x] [Checked documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I created an issue at or, even better, I submitted a pull request to the documentation repository.

summary: Change Open AI to Open AI  (or API Compatible)

description.text: Closes https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/13585 This PR changes the the OpenAI label to OpenAI (Or Api Provider) inside the AI preference tab in the provider dropdown menu. Moreover, when generating the AI Summary, in the processing page, the message has ben changed to use the model selected. ### Steps to test 1- Go to Preference 2- Select AI Tab 3- Select OpenAPI(or Api Compatible) 4- Mark the expert setting 5- Deploy a LLM locally through LM Studio o llama.cpp 6- Add the corresponding url in the field 7- Import a PDF and into the SummaryTab while is being processed you will see the summary is being genetated by the model selected. 8- Moreover, in the AI Chat at the bottom also you could notice that the chat is being powered by Opend API (or Api compatible) ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [x] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [/] Tests created for changes (if applicable) - [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required) - [x] Screenshots added in PR description (if change is visible to the user) - [x] [Checked developer's documentation](https://devdocs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I outlined it in this pull request. - [/] [Checked documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I created an issue at or, even better, I submitted a pull request to the documentation repository.

summary: Updated architecture and components link in CONTRIBUTING.md

description.text: Closes #13586 In the pull request process for CONTRIBUTING.md, the steps previously linked to high-level documentation. Needed to be updated to architecture and components and its corresponding link. ### Steps to test To test this feature, check if the link to leads to the the architecture and components devdocs page when reviewing the 'Pull Request Process' section on CONTRIBUTING.md. Note: While I did see the mention for the .md link, I used .html since CONTRIBUTING.md is primarily viewed on Github. Users clicking on .md link would receive a 404 error. This link format matches other documentation links in the same file. If any changes are needed, or if there any issues due to this I am welcome to change it accordingly. Users should see the above screenshot when clicking the corresponding link. ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [/] Change in `CHANGELOG.md` described in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [/] Tests created for changes (if applicable) - [x] Manually tested changed features in running JabRef (always required) - [x] Screenshots added in PR description (if change is visible to the user) - [x] [Checked developer's documentation](https://devdocs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I outlined it in this pull request. - [x] [Checked documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? If not, I created an issue at or, even better, I submitted a pull request to the documentation repository.

summary: Fix setting of proxy without password

description.text:
Follow-up to #10044

Fixes https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12412

### Steps to test

1. Be in a network requiring a proxy without (!) a password
2. Confgiure JabRef
3. Use citation relations

### Mandatory checks

Go through the checklist below. It is mandatory, even for a draft pull request.

Keep ALL the items. Replace the dots inside [.] and mark them as follows:

-->
